location.office_time_required: optional office access in Irvine, CA

level_signals: Comp range $230K-$248K base consistent with Staff-level IC
Cross-functional leadership across multiple product teams

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of product management experience, with at least 2 years building and shipping conversational AI products
at least 2 years building and shipping conversational AI products
Ability to define a clear product strategy and translate it into a roadmap that balances near-term execution with strategic bets, moving quickly and adapting as the AI landscape evolves

Deep understanding of conversational AI systems, including prompt engineering, memory, guardrails, and evals, with enough technical fluency to ask the right questions and make informed tradeoffs with engineering
Sharp data instincts: knowing which metrics matter, analyzing performance with depth, and using data to drive decisions and hold teams accountable
Strong executive presence and communication skills, including the ability to align teams, build organizational buy-in, and present effectively to senior leadership and the CEO
Hands-on with AI tools, using them actively to prototype ideas, stress-test assumptions, and accelerate the product development process
Bias for speed and action; able to make decisions with incomplete information and move quickly while maintaining the rigor a regulated environment requires
Heart-led and customer-obsessed, bringing genuine empathy to the work and a deep curiosity about the financial lives, hopes, and fears of everyday Americans

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Exceptional drive and precision in delivery
A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission
Optimistic about the potential of societal change

scope.surface: Ask Acorns — AI-powered conversational assistant across Acorns and Acorns Early brands
core_responsibilities: Define how Ask Acorns evolves as an intelligence layer across Acorns and Acorns Early brands
Define and drive the Ask Acorns roadmap
Partner with design to craft Ask Acorns' voice, tone, and persona
Build the strategy for Ask Acorns' skills and capabilities including memory, proactivity, conversation threading, and guardrails
Partner with engineering to establish quality and performance bar including latency and reliability
Define success metrics and eval infrastructure; build customer feedback loops
Use conversation transcripts and system instrumentation to detect issues and improve over time
Drive new ways of working across teams to improve quality and speed of AI product development

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($230K-$248K base) and competitive for Staff PM level
Remote-first organization with optional office access — true remote flexibility
Clear, well-defined product surface (Ask Acorns) with specific capability areas named
Equity (stock options) and comprehensive benefits including 401(k) matching disclosed
JD explicitly states candidates are not expected to meet all listed requirements
High-visibility role with direct executive exposure including CEO
red_flags: E-Verify participation strongly implies no visa sponsorship, though not stated explicitly — work authorization status for non-citizens is ambiguous
10+ year PM requirement with 2+ years specifically in conversational AI is a narrow combined bar
Several cultural-fit requirements ('belief your work is tied to your life's mission', 'optimistic about societal change') may signal a high-intensity culture or mission-alignment filtering

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level inferred from title ('Staff'), comp range, and scope of cross-functional ownership
location.work_auth inferred from E-Verify participation mentioned in Work Authorization section; 'no-sponsorship' not explicitly stated
location.visa_sponsorship inferred as 'no' from E-Verify language; not explicitly confirmed
scope.business_model inferred from product and company description
scope.primary_metrics_inferred — not stated verbatim in JD
compensation.location_pay_differential inferred from JD language 'depending on multiple individualized factors, including market location'
ambiguities: Work authorization: JD mentions E-Verify participation but does not explicitly say 'no sponsorship.' Treated as no-sponsorship given E-Verify framing, but candidates should confirm with recruiter.

Several bullets under 'What you will bring' are written as unconditional requirements but the JD footer says 'You are not expected to have experience with all listed requirements.' Cultural fit items classified as soft; core PM and AI requirements classified as hard.
